name: Spring Bank
description_short: Spring Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank headquartered in Brookfield, Wisconsin, offering personal and business banking services with a focus on customer relationships.
description_long:
Spring Bank is a Wisconsin-based community bank operating from Brookfield with a stated mission centered on customer relationships rather than transactional banking. The bank is FDIC-insured with Certificate ID 123 and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit insurance protection for eligible accounts.

The bank offers both personal and business banking services. On the personal side, they provide deposit accounts including a High Yield Money Market Account with competitive rates, along with standard checking and savings products. For business customers, Spring Bank offers business banking accounts, cash management services, and commercial lending. The bank participated in over $10.7 billion in small business lending across Wisconsin in 2024 and has been recognized as one of Wisconsin's fastest-growing banks by real estate lending.

Spring Bank distinguishes itself through community involvement and recognition. Leadership, including President and CEO Heather Nelson, has received multiple awards including being named a 2026 Power Player by the Milwaukee Business Journal and a Notable Woman in Banking. The bank actively participates in community initiatives including partnerships with the Federal Home Loan Bank of Chicago, United Way fundraising, and golf classics supporting local organizations. The bank has also received recognition as one of the top 20 fastest-growing Wisconsin banks.

As a community bank, Spring Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized banking relationships in the Wisconsin area, particularly those with business lending needs or who value local community involvement. However, the website provides limited detailed information about specific product terms, rates, fees, or the full scope of services available, requiring direct contact for comprehensive product details. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Cash management services for business customers
Commercial lending and small business loans
Deposit rate products with competitive rates
Lost or stolen credit card reporting
Identity theft protection resources and security information
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) services and public file
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with government backing and Certificate ID 123 available for verification
Offers both personal and business banking with specialized cash management services for commercial clients
High Yield Money Market Account available with competitive rates compared to market standards
Strong community focus with partnerships including Federal Home Loan Bank of Chicago and local United Way support
Leadership recognized by Milwaukee Business Journal as among fastest-growing banks and notable banking professionals
Participated in $10.7 billion in small business lending across Wisconsin in 2024
Electronic Deposit Insurance Estimator (EDIE) tool available to help customers understand coverage limits
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a Wisconsin-focused community bank may not serve customers outside the region
Website lacks detailed information about specific account terms, fee schedules, and product offerings
No information provided about online banking features, mobile app capabilities, or digital service offerings
Minimal transparency regarding current deposit rates on the public website - requires navigation to separate rate pages
No mention of credit card products, personal loans, or other consumer lending products beyond business banking

contact.address: 16620 W Bluemound Rd Ste 100A, Brookfield, WI 53005

best_for: Wisconsin-based small business owners seeking personalized commercial banking and cash management services
Local residents prioritizing community-focused banking relationships over national bank chains
Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts with competitive rates in the Brookfield area
diagnosis: Spring Bank is best for Wisconsin-based small business owners and local residents seeking personalized community banking with FDIC-backed deposit protection. The primary caveat is that detailed product information, specific rates, and terms are not fully transparent on the website, requiring direct customer contact to compare offerings against national or online-only banking alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
